purpose.summary: This COBOL program contains routines for inserting child segments into a GSAM database, closing files, and handling abends. It uses IMS calls via CBLTDLI to interact with the database.

called_programs.purpose: To perform IMS database operations, specifically inserting a child segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph inserts a child segment into a GSAM database using the IMS call 'CBLTDLI'. It uses the 'FUNC-ISRT' function code, 'PADFLPCB' as the PCB, and 'PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS' as the segment to be inserted. After the call, it checks the 'PADFL-PCB-STATUS' field for errors. If the status is not spaces, it displays error messages including the PCB status and KFB area, and then performs 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. The paragraph consumes data from PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS and PADFLPCB, and calls CBLTDLI to write to the GSAM database. It handles error conditions by checking the PCB status and calling the abend routine.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 3200-INSERT-CHILD-SEG-GSAM paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement to return control to the calling paragraph. It does not consume any inputs or produce any outputs. It does not implement any business logic or error handling. It is a standard practice for structured COBOL programming to have an explicit exit paragraph.

This paragraph is responsible for closing files used by the program. Currently, it only displays a message 'CLOSING THE FILE'. It does not take any inputs or produce any specific outputs other than the display message. It does not implement any business logic or error handling beyond the display message. The specific files to be closed are not evident from this snippet, suggesting the actual close statements are elsewhere or missing.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 4000-FILE-CLOSE paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement to return control to the calling paragraph. It does not consume any inputs or produce any outputs. It does not implement any business logic or error handling. It is a standard practice for structured COBOL programming to have an explicit exit paragraph.

This paragraph handles program termination (abend) scenarios. It displays a message 'DBUNLDGS ABENDING ...', sets the RETURN-CODE to 16, and then terminates the program using GOBACK. It does not take any specific inputs, but it sets the RETURN-CODE as output. It does not implement any business logic beyond setting the return code. It is called when a critical error occurs that prevents the program from continuing, such as a failed IMS call.

This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 9999-ABEND paragraph. It contains a simple EXIT statement to return control to the calling paragraph. It does not consume any inputs or produce any outputs. It does not implement any business logic or error handling. It is a standard practice for structured COBOL programming to have an explicit exit paragraph.
